entries.message: ForceGraph2D d3Force configuration requires importing d3-force and using fg.d3Force() after graph mounts via useEffect. Key insight: heterogeneous node types need per-node charge/collision calculations, not global settings.
When filtering heterogeneous graph data, concepts have 'domains' (array) while thoughts have 'domain' (string) - filtering logic must handle both cases explicitly with type checks.

When using force-graph with data that has pre-set fx/fy positions, you MUST clear them if you want dynamic layout. Otherwise nodes with fixed positions anchor the simulation and unfixed nodes cluster at origin.

When thoughts connect to multiple concepts, they create indirect pulling between concepts via the link force. A radial force explicitly pushing concepts outward (strength 0.8, radius 250) is more effective than relying on charge repulsion alone.

Physics-only force layouts CANNOT distribute hub nodes when leaf nodes connect to multiple hubs. The link forces pull hubs together through shared leaves. Solution: PIN hubs using fx/fy and only let leaves move.

Dynamic centering pattern: zoomToFit alone may not properly center the graph. Use zoomToFit followed by centerAt(centroidX, centroidY) after a timeout (450ms) for reliable centering. Calculate centroid from all nodes with valid x/y coordinates.

Edge-to-edge canvas requires: (1) dimensionsRef to track viewport at data-fetch time, (2) responsive grid spacing calculated from dimensionsRef.current.width/height * 0.85, (3) zoomToFit with padding 0 (not 5 or 10). All three zoomToFit calls must use padding 0.
